speaker:
BISHOP
               
              OF
               
              CANTERBURY
text: My lord, I’ll tell you that self bill is urged

speaker:
BISHOP
               
              OF
               
              CANTERBURY
text: Which in th’ eleventh year of the last king’s reign

speaker:
BISHOP
               
              OF
               
              CANTERBURY
text: Was like, and had indeed against us passed

speaker:
BISHOP
               
              OF
               
              CANTERBURY
text: But that the scambling and unquiet time

speaker:
BISHOP
               
              OF
               
              CANTERBURY
text: Did push it out of farther question.

speaker:
BISHOP
               
              OF
               
              ELY
text: But how, my lord, shall we resist it now?

speaker:
BISHOP
               
              OF
               
              CANTERBURY
text: It must be thought on. If it pass against us,

speaker:
BISHOP
               
              OF
               
              CANTERBURY
text: We lose the better half of our possession,

speaker:
BISHOP
               
              OF
               
              CANTERBURY
text: For all the temporal lands which men devout

speaker:
BISHOP
               
              OF
               
              CANTERBURY
text: By testament have given to the Church

speaker:
BISHOP
               
              OF
               
              CANTERBURY
text: Would they strip from us, being valued thus:

speaker:
BISHOP
               
              OF
               
              CANTERBURY
text: “As much as would maintain, to the King’s honor,

speaker:
BISHOP
               
              OF
               
              CANTERBURY
text: Full fifteen earls and fifteen hundred knights,

speaker:
BISHOP
               
              OF
               
              CANTERBURY
text: Six thousand and two hundred good esquires;

speaker:
BISHOP
               
              OF
               
              CANTERBURY
text: And, to relief of lazars and weak age

speaker:
BISHOP
               
              OF
               
              CANTERBURY
text: Of indigent faint souls past corporal toil,

speaker:
BISHOP
               
              OF
               
              CANTERBURY
text: A hundred almshouses right well supplied;

speaker:
BISHOP
               
              OF
               
              CANTERBURY
text: And to the coffers of the King besides,

speaker:
BISHOP
               
              OF
               
              CANTERBURY
text: A thousand pounds by th’ year.” Thus runs the bill.

speaker:
BISHOP
               
              OF
               
              ELY
text: This would drink deep.

speaker:
BISHOP
               
              OF
               
              CANTERBURY
text: ’Twould drink the cup and all.

speaker:
BISHOP
               
              OF
               
              ELY
text: But what prevention?

speaker:
BISHOP
               
              OF
               
              CANTERBURY
text: The King is full of grace and fair regard.

speaker:
BISHOP
               
              OF
               
              ELY
text: And a true lover of the holy Church.

speaker:
BISHOP
               
              OF
               
              CANTERBURY
text: The courses of his youth promised it not.

speaker:
BISHOP
               
              OF
               
              CANTERBURY
text: The breath no sooner left his father’s body

speaker:
BISHOP
               
              OF
               
              CANTERBURY
text: But that his wildness, mortified in him,

speaker:
BISHOP
               
              OF
               
              CANTERBURY
text: Seemed to die too. Yea, at that very moment

speaker:
BISHOP
               
              OF
               
              CANTERBURY
text: Consideration like an angel came

speaker:
BISHOP
               
              OF
               
              CANTERBURY
text: And whipped th’ offending Adam out of him,

speaker:
BISHOP
               
              OF
               
              CANTERBURY
text: Leaving his body as a paradise

speaker:
BISHOP
               
              OF
               
              CANTERBURY
text: T’ envelop and contain celestial spirits.

speaker:
BISHOP
               
              OF
               
              CANTERBURY
text: Never was such a sudden scholar made,

speaker:
BISHOP
               
              OF
               
              CANTERBURY
text: Never came reformation in a flood

speaker:
BISHOP
               
              OF
               
              CANTERBURY
text: With such a heady currance scouring faults,

speaker:
BISHOP
               
              OF
               
              CANTERBURY
text: Nor never Hydra-headed willfulness

speaker:
BISHOP
               
              OF
               
              CANTERBURY
text: So soon did lose his seat, and all at once,

speaker:
BISHOP
               
              OF
               
              CANTERBURY
text: As in this king.

speaker:
BISHOP
               
              OF
               
              ELY
text: We are blessèd in the change.

speaker:
BISHOP
               
              OF
               
              CANTERBURY
text: Hear him but reason in divinity

speaker:
BISHOP
               
              OF
               
              CANTERBURY
text: And, all-admiring, with an inward wish

speaker:
BISHOP
               
              OF
               
              CANTERBURY
text: You would desire the King were made a prelate;

speaker:
BISHOP
               
              OF
               
              CANTERBURY
text: Hear him debate of commonwealth affairs,

speaker:
BISHOP
               
              OF
               
              CANTERBURY
text: You would say it hath been all in all his study;

speaker:
BISHOP
               
              OF
               
              CANTERBURY
text: List his discourse of war, and you shall hear

speaker:
BISHOP
               
              OF
               
              CANTERBURY
text: A fearful battle rendered you in music;

speaker:
BISHOP
               
              OF
               
              CANTERBURY
text: Turn him to any cause of policy,

speaker:
BISHOP
               
              OF
               
              CANTERBURY
text: The Gordian knot of it he will unloose

speaker:
BISHOP
               
              OF
               
              CANTERBURY
text: Familiar as his garter; that, when he speaks,

speaker:
BISHOP
               
              OF
               
              CANTERBURY
text: The air, a chartered libertine, is still,

speaker:
BISHOP
               
              OF
               
              CANTERBURY
text: And the mute wonder lurketh in men’s ears

speaker:
BISHOP
               
              OF
               
              CANTERBURY
text: To steal his sweet and honeyed sentences;

speaker:
BISHOP
               
              OF
               
              CANTERBURY
text: So that the art and practic part of life

speaker:
BISHOP
               
              OF
               
              CANTERBURY
text: Must be the mistress to this theoric;

speaker:
BISHOP
               
              OF
               
              CANTERBURY
text: Which is a wonder how his Grace should glean it,

speaker:
BISHOP
               
              OF
               
              CANTERBURY
text: Since his addiction was to courses vain,

speaker:
BISHOP
               
              OF
               
              CANTERBURY
text: His companies unlettered, rude, and shallow,

speaker:
BISHOP
               
              OF
               
              CANTERBURY
text: His hours filled up with riots, banquets, sports,

speaker:
BISHOP
               
              OF
               
              CANTERBURY
text: And never noted in him any study,

speaker:
BISHOP
               
              OF
               
              CANTERBURY
text: Any retirement, any sequestration

speaker:
BISHOP
               
              OF
               
              CANTERBURY
text: From open haunts and popularity.

speaker:
BISHOP
               
              OF
               
              ELY
text: The strawberry grows underneath the nettle,

speaker:
BISHOP
               
              OF
               
              ELY
text: And wholesome berries thrive and ripen best

speaker:
BISHOP
               
              OF
               
              ELY
text: Neighbored by fruit of baser quality;

speaker:
BISHOP
               
              OF
               
              ELY
text: And so the Prince obscured his contemplation

speaker:
BISHOP
               
              OF
               
              ELY
text: Under the veil of wildness, which, no doubt,

speaker:
BISHOP
               
              OF
               
              ELY
text: Grew like the summer grass, fastest by night,

speaker:
BISHOP
               
              OF
               
              ELY
text: Unseen yet crescive in his faculty.

speaker:
BISHOP
               
              OF
               
              CANTERBURY
text: It must be so, for miracles are ceased,

speaker:
BISHOP
               
              OF
               
              CANTERBURY
text: And therefore we must needs admit the means

speaker:
BISHOP
               
              OF
               
              CANTERBURY
text: How things are perfected.

speaker:
BISHOP
               
              OF
               
              ELY
text: But, my good lord,

speaker:
BISHOP
               
              OF
               
              ELY
text: How now for mitigation of this bill

speaker:
BISHOP
               
              OF
               
              ELY
text: Urged by the Commons? Doth his Majesty

speaker:
BISHOP
               
              OF
               
              ELY
text: Incline to it or no?

speaker:
BISHOP
               
              OF
               
              CANTERBURY
text: He seems indifferent,

speaker:
BISHOP
               
              OF
               
              CANTERBURY
text: Or rather swaying more upon our part

speaker:
BISHOP
               
              OF
               
              CANTERBURY
text: Than cherishing th’ exhibitors against us;

speaker:
BISHOP
               
              OF
               
              CANTERBURY
text: For I have made an offer to his Majesty—

speaker:
BISHOP
               
              OF
               
              CANTERBURY
text: Upon our spiritual convocation

speaker:
BISHOP
               
              OF
               
              CANTERBURY
text: And in regard of causes now in hand,

speaker:
BISHOP
               
              OF
               
              CANTERBURY
text: Which I have opened to his Grace at large,

speaker:
BISHOP
               
              OF
               
              CANTERBURY
text: As touching France—to give a greater sum

speaker:
BISHOP
               
              OF
               
              CANTERBURY
text: Than ever at one time the clergy yet

speaker:
BISHOP
               
              OF
               
              CANTERBURY
text: Did to his predecessors part withal.

speaker:
BISHOP
               
              OF
               
              ELY
text: How did this offer seem received, my lord?

speaker:
BISHOP
               
              OF
               
              CANTERBURY
text: With good acceptance of his Majesty—

speaker:
BISHOP
               
              OF
               
              CANTERBURY
text: Save that there was not time enough to hear,

speaker:
BISHOP
               
              OF
               
              CANTERBURY
text: As I perceived his Grace would fain have done,

speaker:
BISHOP
               
              OF
               
              CANTERBURY
text: The severals and unhidden passages

speaker:
BISHOP
               
              OF
               
              CANTERBURY
text: Of his true titles to some certain dukedoms,

speaker:
BISHOP
               
              OF
               
              CANTERBURY
text: And generally to the crown and seat of France,

speaker:
BISHOP
               
              OF
               
              CANTERBURY
text: Derived from Edward, his great-grandfather.

speaker:
BISHOP
               
              OF
               
              ELY
text: What was th’ impediment that broke this off?

speaker:
BISHOP
               
              OF
               
              CANTERBURY
text: The French ambassador upon that instant

speaker:
BISHOP
               
              OF
               
              CANTERBURY
text: Craved audience. And the hour, I think, is come

speaker:
BISHOP
               
              OF
               
              CANTERBURY
text: To give him hearing. Is it four o’clock?

speaker:
BISHOP
               
              OF
               
              ELY
text: It is.

speaker:
BISHOP
               
              OF
               
              CANTERBURY
text: Then go we in to know his embassy,

speaker:
BISHOP
               
              OF
               
              CANTERBURY
text: Which I could with a ready guess declare

speaker:
BISHOP
               
              OF
               
              CANTERBURY
text: Before the Frenchman speak a word of it.

speaker:
BISHOP
               
              OF
               
              ELY
text: I’ll wait upon you, and I long to hear it.
